description: A baked fruit pie made with apples, sugar, and spices inside a pastry crust. It is best known for its sweet-tart filling and flaky top or double crust.
optional_ingredients: Cheddar cheese or ice cream for serving
preparation_steps: Prepare or chill the pie dough.
Peel and slice the apples, then toss them with sugar, spices, acid, and thickener.
Line a pie dish with pastry and fill it with the apple mixture.
Add a top crust or lattice, seal the edges, and vent the top.
Bake until the crust is browned and the filling is bubbling, then cool before slicing.

nutrition.calories: Typically moderate to high per slice

health_advice.benefits: Provides some fiber and micronutrients from apples, especially when part of a balanced diet.
health_advice.risks: Often high in sugar and refined flour, and can be energy-dense when made with a buttery crust.
health_advice.healthy_tip: Use more fruit, less added sugar, and moderate portions; serving it without heavy toppings keeps it lighter.

dietary_notes.note: Traditional versions are vegetarian. Vegan versions need dairy-free fat, and gluten-free versions need a gluten-free crust.

allergens: Egg if egg wash or egg-based pastry is used
common_variations: Dutch apple pie with crumb topping
